hero.subtitle: AI-powered coding challenges. From zero to fluent in any programming topic — in minutes.

ai.title: AI Tutor
ai.description: An AI that understands your level and adapts in real time. No cookie-cutter explanations.

editor.title: Live Editor
editor.description: Write, run, and validate code without leaving the browser. Instant feedback on every keystroke.

progressive.title: Progressive
progressive.description: Each challenge builds on the last. Track your progress and master topics step by step.

apiKeys.title: Use your own AI model
apiKeys.description: Link your Anthropic or OpenAI key to use your favorite models for tutorial generation.

step1.title: Pick a topic
step1.description: Type anything — a framework, a concept, an algorithm. CodeQuest generates a tailored challenge in seconds.

step2.title: Write real code
step2.description: No multiple choice. You write actual code in the editor, against real test cases.

step3.title: Get smarter feedback
step3.description: The AI reviews your solution, explains what you missed, and suggests the next step.

editorPreview.label: The editor
editorPreview.description: No drag-and-drop blocks. No fill-in-the-blank. You write code in a real editor with syntax highlighting, autocomplete, and live test output.
editorPreview.feature2: Instant test runner with detailed output

finalCta.headline: READY TO CODE?
finalCta.subtitle: No credit card. No setup. Just pick a topic.

footer.tagline: AI-powered coding challenges for developers who want to actually learn.

dashboard.title: Your Learning Journey
dashboard.subtitle: Track your progress and continue mastering new technologies.

complete.heading: Tutorial Complete
complete.message: You've finished all the steps. Regenerate this tutorial to unlock the final project challenge.

output.label: Output
output.placeholder: Run your code to see the output here...

errors.accountWithDifferentProvider: You already have an account with this email. Try logging in with Google.
errors.userNotFound: No account found with this email.
errors.weakPassword: Password must be at least 6 characters.
errors.generic: An error occurred. Please try again.

notFound.subtitle: Page not found
notFound.message: The page you are looking for doesn't exist or has been removed.

error.title: Oops!
error.subtitle: Something went wrong
error.message: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again or go back to the home page.
error.rateLimit: Rate limit exceeded. Please wait a moment and try again.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage your API keys and preferred model.

devlog.title: Dev Log
devlog.subtitle: CodeQuest development journey — decisions, bugs and strategy changes.

setup.title: Initial project setup
setup.description: Project initialized with Vite + React + TypeScript. TailwindCSS configured with custom theme (background, surface, amber), Firebase, TanStack Router with file-based routing and TanStack Query.

data_layer.title: Data layer — types, hooks, stores and AI services
data_layer.description: Foundation created: TypeScript types for Tutorial and Step, useAuth hook with Firebase Google Login, Zustand store for the editor, and first AI service (Gemini) for generating structured JSON tutorials.
decisions: Zustand chosen for local UI state — simpler than Context for synchronized state
Gemini as default model because it's free, with Claude and OpenAI support via user-provided keys

routing.title: Routing layer — root, index, tutorial and profile
routing.description: Route structure with TanStack Router: __root.tsx with QueryClientProvider and AuthProvider, dynamic tutorial route (/tutorial/$id), and profile route.

queries.title: Query layer — TanStack Query + Firestore
queries.description: Queries implemented with TanStack Query: AI tutorial generation, user profile fetch from Firestore, and per-tutorial progress. Query options pattern for reuse.

tailwind_config.title: ESLint, fonts and Tailwind theme configuration
tailwind_config.description: JetBrains Mono as monospace font, Inter as sans-serif. Color tokens defined via CSS custom properties in Tailwind v4 @theme: background #0d0f14, surface #13161e, amber #f5a623.
decisions: CSS custom properties in @theme (Tailwind v4) instead of tailwind.config.js — no separate config file needed
JetBrains Mono chosen as it is the logo font and used in code blocks

components.title: Layout, tutorial and editor components
components.description: Component layer created: Header with navigation, TutorialStep with ConceptBlock and ChallengeBlock, MonacoWrapper for code editing and OutputPanel for result display.

ui_refactor.title: UI refactor — full-viewport layout and Run/Verify editor
ui_refactor.description: Redesigned to full-viewport layout: left content panel + right editor panel side-by-side. Separate Run and Verify buttons. Collapsible StepNav.

animations.title: Animations with Motion
animations.description: Animated transitions between tutorial steps using Motion (AnimatePresence). Completion button with visual feedback.

dashboard.title: Dashboard and Firestore tutorial persistence
dashboard.description: Route /dashboard with a grid of tutorials generated by the user. Persistence in Firestore with subcollection /users/{uid}/tutorials. Per-step progress with markStepComplete.
bugs: Permission denied on saveTutorial: match /tutorials/{id} rule was nested in the wrong block (outside /users/{userId})
Permission denied on markStepComplete: match /progress/{id} block accidentally removed from security rules
isValidUserProgress failing: serverTimestamp() cannot be validated as 'is timestamp' in rules — validator removed

left_panel.title: Light left panel with Example and Challenge sections
left_panel.description: Tutorial content panel with light background (amber-50) for the example block and dark background kept on the challenge block — visually differentiating the two content types. 'Example' and 'Challenge' labels added. Divider between blocks.
decisions: ChallengeBlock keeps dark background to differentiate from the example — user suggestion

auth.title: Multi-provider auth: GitHub + email, animated drawer and avatar
auth.description: LoginDrawer with animated slide-in (Motion). Support for Google, GitHub and email/password. Avatar in Header with fallback to name initial. On sign out: editorStore is reset and user is redirected to /.
bugs: GitHubIcon does not exist in lucide-react — replaced with inline SVG
Name mismatch: hook exports signInWithGitHub, component was calling signInWithGithub
React.FormEvent deprecated — replaced with React.SyntheticEvent<HTMLFormElement>
Duplicate state in LoginDrawer: had individual name/email/password states AND formData — individual states removed
onChange={() => handleChange} in Form.tsx — correct is onChange={handleChange}
Avatar broke when photoURL exists but image fails — added onError handler and imgError state
auth/operation-not-allowed for email — Email/Password was not enabled in Firebase Console
auth/account-exists-with-different-credential — handling added to friendlyError()
decisions: authStore (Zustand) created to control drawer open/close from any component

cloudinary.title: Photo upload via Cloudinary and profile editing
cloudinary.description: Profile form with photo upload, name editing and API key configuration (Anthropic, OpenAI) and preferred model. Photo sent to Cloudinary via unsigned upload preset.
decisions: Cloudinary chosen over Firebase Storage — Firebase Storage requires Blaze plan (paid), Cloudinary has a generous free tier

landing_redesign.title: Landing page redesign with component architecture
landing_redesign.description: Landing page refactored from a 183-line file into 5 independent components: HeroBadge, HeroHeadline, TopicSearch, ApiKeysCard and FeatureCards. Larger and more impactful typography.

ai_verify.title: AI solution verification and language auto-detection
ai_verify.description: The 'Verify Solution' button sends code + challenge to the user's configured model (Gemini/Claude/OpenAI). Feedback shown in OutputPanel with status: correct (green), partial (amber) or incorrect (red). Monaco language auto-detected from tutorial topic.
bugs: Monaco did not natively support TypeScript via new Function() — added sucrase to transpile before execution
decisions: Run remains manual — user executes the code and then clicks Verify
verifyService decoupled from the component — receives model, keys and code as pure parameters

i18n_feature.title: Internationalization with 6 languages
i18n_feature.description: Support for PT-BR, EN, ES, DE, EL (Greek) and PL (Polish) via react-i18next + i18next-browser-languagedetector. 122 strings extracted from 22 files. LanguageSwitcher dropdown in Header. Automatic browser language detection.

error_screen.title: Error screen and 404 page
error_screen.description: ErrorScreen inspired by Colorlib-404-3 design: giant 'Oops!' title with text-shadow for 3D effect, entry animations, optional technical message and Back/Retry buttons. Integrated as errorComponent in root route and tutorial route. NotFound with same visual for 404.

polish.title: Cursor pointer, stronger hover effects and 429 error handling
polish.description: cursor:pointer and brightness(1.12) on global hover for all buttons and links. active:scale(0.97) for press feedback. 429 (rate limit) errors caught in AI services and displayed with a friendly message.

tests.title: Vitest setup with unit tests
tests.description: Vitest configured with jsdom and @testing-library/jest-dom. vitest.config.ts separated from vite.config.ts (type incompatibility between Vite 6/rolldown and Vitest). Tests for serviceErrors, NotFound, ErrorScreen and friendlyError.
bugs: defineConfig from vitest/config conflicts with Vite 6 (rolldown) plugins — required as any on plugins
getByText('404') fails because each digit is a separate <span> — solution: textContent matcher
vi.mock('react-i18next') incomplete: i18n/index.ts calls initReactI18next which was missing from the mock
decisions: vitest.config.ts separated from vite.config.ts to avoid Vite 6 type conflicts
Only the react() plugin in vitest.config — tanstackRouter and tailwindcss are build plugins, unnecessary in tests

design_system.title: Design system page
design_system.description: Route /design with visual token documentation: color palette with swatches, typographic scale (sans and mono), font weights, button variants, badges, inputs, cards, spacing and border radius.

herobadge.title: HeroBadge repositioned to the Header
herobadge.description: Badge 'AI-Powered Interactive Learning' moved to sit next to the logo in the Header. Behavior: displays only the icon with a border; hover expands the text with a max-width animation. Previously centered in the landing page, taking up unnecessary space.

mux_layout.title: Mux-inspired layout redesign
mux_layout.description: Complete visual overhaul of all pages and shared components inspired by Mux's design language: sharp edges, border-grid layouts with divide-x/divide-y, uppercase font-black typography, and full-width sections with max-w-7xl containers. Added /mux route as a visual reference.
decisions: Adopted border dividers instead of shadows and rounded cards to reinforce an engineering-first aesthetic
ProfileForm, Dashboard, and Design pages migrated to the same full-bleed section pattern as the landing page

step_count_fix.title: Fix: AI step count override
step_count_fix.description: All three AI services (Gemini, Claude, OpenAI) appended "Generate exactly 5 steps" to the prompt after SYSTEM_PROMPT, silently overriding the "at least 10" instruction. Removed the override from all services.

dynamic_header.title: Dynamic route-aware header
dynamic_header.description: Header now adapts per route: hides the Dashboard icon on /dashboard, collapses the logo to CQ on tutorial routes, and renders the StepNav in the center slot via a dedicated Zustand store (tutorialNavStore).
decisions: Created tutorialNavStore to share step data between TutorialPage and Header without prop drilling through the root layout
StepNav moved from the tutorial page body into the Header center slot, freeing vertical space for the editor

dead_code_cleanup.title: Dead code removal and centralized constants
dead_code_cleanup.description: Deleted mux.tsx (prototype route) and TopicSearch.tsx (replaced by TopicSearchForm). Created src/constants/topics.ts with SUGGESTED_TOPICS and TECH_STRIP, eliminating duplication across landing components. Removed debug console.log calls from LoginDrawer and aiService.

utils_extraction.title: Extract detectLanguage, getTimeAgo and parseAiJson to utils
utils_extraction.description: detectLanguage() moved from tutorial.$id.tsx, getTimeAgo() from dashboard.tsx, and parseAiJson() extracted from geminiService and verifyService into a shared src/utils/ directory. parseAiJson eliminates duplicated markdown-cleanup + JSON.parse logic. Barrel export at src/utils/index.ts.
decisions: getTimeAgo() accepts TFunction as a parameter to keep i18n working without coupling the util to the hook

ui_primitives.title: UI primitives, ProfileForm split and dashboard component extraction
ui_primitives.description: Created src/components/ui/ with Input and Button primitives. ProfileForm split into ProfileInputs, ProfileSelectInput, ImageInput and ProfileHeader subcomponents. Fixed a duplicate saveProfile() call that was saving twice on every submit. TutorialCard extracted from dashboard.tsx into its own file with proper Tutorial typing, removing all Record<string, unknown> casts.
decisions: Button padding left as a className prop — context varies too much (px-4/px-6/px-8) to hardcode in the variant

tutorial_type_fix.title: Fix Tutorial type and TutorialCard stepCount for legacy Firestore docs
tutorial_type_fix.description: Tutorial interface updated: level added, steps made optional, stepCount added for backwards-compatibility with documents saved before step persistence was introduced, createdAt typed as { seconds: number } | null to match Firestore Timestamp shape. TutorialCard reads stepCount ?? steps?.length ?? 0 so both old and new documents display correctly.
bugs: Tutoriais gerados antes da feature de persistência de steps não tinham o campo steps, causando erro 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length)' ao abrir o Dashboard

level_system.title: Level selector, tutorial persistence and deterministic IDs
level_system.description: Level type (beginner/intermediate/advanced) added. buildSystemPrompt() adapts instructions per difficulty. All AI services receive and forward the level param. Tutorial IDs are deterministic: ${slug}-${level}, so the same topic at different levels creates separate Firestore documents. getTutorial checks Firestore before calling AI, eliminating redundant generation on revisit.
decisions: Deterministic IDs chosen over random UUIDs to enable Firestore-first caching per topic+level combination
Level stored in search params (?level=beginner) so the URL is shareable and bookmarkable

landing_i18n.title: Landing page refactored into focused components with full i18n
landing_i18n.description: 495-line index.tsx split into 15 focused components: HeroHeadline, LevelSelector, TopicSearchForm, SuggestedTopics, CodeSnippetDemo, FeatureCards, HowItWorks, StatsBar, EditorPreview, TechStrip, FinalCTA and LandingFooter. All hardcoded text replaced with t() calls. Six new i18n sections added to all 6 locales: howItWorks, stats, editorPreview, finalCta, techStrip, footer.

editor_format.title: Format button and language guard in Monaco editor
editor_format.description: EditorToolbar extracted as a dedicated component with language badge and Format/Run/Verify buttons. Format uses Monaco's built-in editor.action.formatDocument action. Run is now guarded — CSS, HTML, SQL, JSON and other non-executable languages display a localised message instead of attempting to transpile and failing with a syntax error.

tutorial_flow.title: Fix: tutorial step gate, Monaco remount, error propagation
tutorial_flow.description: Next button disabled until AI verifier returns correct status. Monaco remounts on step change via key={step.id} so defaultValue loads fresh for each step. Editor state resets on transition. Verify errors (including 429 rate limit) now propagate to the feedback panel.
bugs: Monaco retained the previous step's code after advancing — defaultValue only applies on initial mount, not on prop updates
Header covered by content due to mt-10 (40px) being smaller than actual header height (60px)
catch block in handleVerify discarded the error message, showing a generic fallback even for specific API errors

dashboard_cache_fix.title: Fix: Dashboard not showing newly generated tutorials
dashboard_cache_fix.description: After generating a tutorial, the ["tutorials", uid] TanStack Query cache was never invalidated. The Dashboard read stale data (staleTime: 30s) and the new tutorial didn't appear. Fixed by adding a useEffect in TutorialPage that calls invalidateQueries when a tutorial loads. Additionally, saveTutorial was not persisting the level field — fixed by adding level to the Firestore setDoc payload.
bugs: New tutorials not appearing in Dashboard after generation — stale cache was never invalidated
Tutorial level not persisted to Firestore, so Dashboard cards always linked with level: undefined

tutorial_save_fix.title: Fix: Tutorial query firing before auth resolves, createdAt type mismatch
tutorial_save_fix.description: The tutorial query ran on initial render with user?.uid = undefined (auth still loading), so tutorials were generated but never saved to Firestore. Fixed by adding enabled: !loading to the query so it waits for Firebase Auth to resolve. level was also not in the AI response schema — now explicitly injected after generation. Query errors (e.g. 429 rate limit) were silently swallowed, showing an infinite skeleton — fixed by rethrowing the error to bubble up to the route errorComponent. Finally, createdAt was typed as { seconds: number } | null to match Firestore Timestamp shape, but AI services were returning Date.now() (a number) — fixed by returning null from all three services since the real value is set by serverTimestamp() on save.
bugs: Tutorial generated but not saved to Firestore — query fired before auth resolved, uid was undefined
level field missing from saved tutorials — AI schema does not include it, must be injected explicitly
Query errors showed infinite skeleton instead of ErrorScreen — isError state was not handled
createdAt type mismatch — AI services returned Date.now() but Tutorial type expects { seconds: number } | null

progress_bar.title: Progress bar on TutorialCard
progress_bar.description: Each Dashboard card now shows a progress bar with percentage and step count. getAllProgress fetches all progress documents for the user in a single Firestore query (instead of N per card). DashboardContent passes completedSteps[] to each TutorialCard, which calculates percent = completedSteps.length / stepCount.

parse_ai_json_fix.title: Fix: robust JSON extraction from AI responses
parse_ai_json_fix.description: parseAiJson rewritten to extract JSON by finding the outermost { } block instead of stripping markdown fences with regex. The previous approach only removed fences at the exact start of the string — if the model prefixed the response with text like 'Here is your tutorial:', the fence was never removed and JSON.parse received the full raw text.
bugs: Expected ':' after property name — model prefixed the JSON with a sentence, fence regex didn't match, JSON.parse received raw text

sandpack.title: Sandpack live preview for web-based final projects
sandpack.description: FinalProjectView now conditionally renders a Sandpack editor with live preview for web topics (React, HTML, CSS, vanilla JS, Node). getSandpackTemplate maps the tutorial topic to a SandpackPredefinedTemplate; topics without a match (Python, Rust, etc.) continue using Monaco. SandpackProvider wraps a split-pane layout: SandpackCodeEditor on top and SandpackPreview below, both filling available height.
decisions: Template selection is keyword-based (topic.toLowerCase().includes(key)) rather than exact match — handles variations like 'React Hooks' or 'HTML & CSS'
Monaco kept as fallback for non-web topics to avoid blank previews for server-side or systems languages

tutorial_complete.title: Fallback screen when final project is unavailable
tutorial_complete.description: TutorialComplete component added as a fallback when showFinalProject is true but the tutorial document has no finalProject field. This happens with tutorials generated before the v2 prompt schema was introduced. The screen prompts the user to regenerate and provides a back-to-dashboard button.

tutorial_loading.title: Animated loading screen with progressive step indicators
tutorial_loading.description: TutorialLoading replaces TutorialSkeleton during tutorial generation. Displays a vertical checklist of generation phases (Analyzing topic → Structuring curriculum → Writing code examples → Building challenges → Assembling final project) that appear sequentially with randomised delays. The active phase shows a rotating amber spinner; completed phases show a green checkmark. Translated across all 6 locales.

step_nav_fix.title: Fix: step navigation off-by-one and amber active state
step_nav_fix.description: StepNav compared step.order (1-based) directly against currentStep (0-based), causing the active highlight to always point to the wrong step, the title to show for the previous step, and click handlers to never fire for non-completed steps. Fixed to step.order - 1 === currentStep. Active step now always shows its title in amber; inactive steps show only the number badge; completed steps show a checkmark with hover:opacity-70. Removed the motion spring expand animation.
bugs: Step navigation clicked the wrong step — off-by-one between 1-based order and 0-based currentStep index
Two steps appeared checked on load because isActive was never true, preventing the correct visual distinction between active and completed

intro_button.title: Info button to reopen tutorial intro at any time
intro_button.description: An Info icon button added to the header, visible only on tutorial pages. Clicking it calls setShowIntro(true), restoring the TutorialIntro screen from any step. Tutorial route now resets showIntro to true and showFinalProject to false on mount, so revisiting a tutorial always starts from the intro screen.

persist_step_code.title: Persist completed step code to Firestore
persist_step_code.description: markStepComplete now saves the user's editor code under completedCode[stepId] in the progress document. TutorialStep loads the saved code as defaultValue when navigating back to a completed step, falling back to starterCode if no code was saved. updateDoc with dot notation used for nested field writes — setDoc merge did not support dot-notation paths.
bugs: Completed step code was not persisted — setDoc merge treated 'completedCode.step-1' as a literal field name with a dot, not as a nested path

openai_prompt_fix.title: Fix OpenAI prompt structure and enforce exact step count
openai_prompt_fix.description: OpenAI service refactored to send system and user messages separately instead of concatenating them into a single user message. response_format: json_object added to prevent non-JSON output. parseAiJson now used instead of JSON.parse for consistency. systemPrompt updated with a CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS block specifying exact step counts per level (beginner: 8, intermediate: 10, advanced: 12) with 'no more, no less' instruction.
bugs: OpenAI returned significantly poorer tutorial quality — system prompt was sent as part of the user message, losing the system role context
Step count was non-deterministic — AI interpreted the previous 8–10 / 10–12 / 12–15 ranges as suggestions and returned arbitrary counts

final_project_view.title: Final project view with Monaco editor and solution toggle
final_project_view.description: FinalProjectView component added as the capstone screen shown after completing all tutorial steps. Uses the same two-column layout as TutorialStepView — project brief on the left, Monaco editor on the right with the starter code pre-loaded. A Show/Hide Solution toggle reveals the complete solution in a code block. challenge prop on MonacoWrapper made optional (Challenge | null) with a null guard in handleVerify, so the Verify button is safely disabled in the final project context. language prop renamed to monacoLanguage across MonacoWrapper, EditorToolbar and TutorialStepView for clarity.

tutorial_intro.title: Tutorial intro screen, i18n-aware content generation, robust JSON parsing
tutorial_intro.description: TutorialIntro component added as a pre-step screen showing overview, real-world usage, pros/cons and prerequisites before the tutorial starts. showIntro state added to editorStore. Tutorial content language now derived from i18n.language and mapped to readable names (e.g. pt-BR → Brazilian Portuguese) before being passed to buildSystemPrompt — the AI generates content in the user's app language. parseAiJson updated with a fallback that sanitizes literal newlines/tabs inside JSON string values, fixing Bad escaped character errors caused by AI-generated code blocks.
bugs: Bad escaped character in JSON — AI-generated code fields contained literal newlines instead of \n, crashing JSON.parse
detectLanguage (Monaco language) was removed when language param was added, leaving TutorialStepView without a language prop
decisions: Content language sourced from i18n.language instead of a URL search param — the app locale is the single source of truth
parseAiJson uses try/catch with regex sanitization fallback instead of a third-party dirty-json dependency (package had conflicting peer deps)

tutorial_prompt_v2.title: Tutorial prompt v2 — introduction, progressive steps and final project
tutorial_prompt_v2.description: buildSystemPrompt rewritten to enforce a three-part structure: (1) Introduction with overview, real-world usage, pros/cons and prerequisites; (2) hands-on steps with level-specific depth and count guidelines; (3) a capstone final project integrating all concepts. levelInstructions expanded with explicit audience, step count, code depth and project complexity per level. verifyService.buildPrompt translated to English so all AI prompts are consistent. Tutorial type updated with optional introduction and finalProject fields.
decisions: introduction and finalProject marked as optional in the Tutorial interface for backwards compatibility with existing Firestore documents
verifyService feedback language made dynamic ('same language as the challenge prompt') instead of hardcoded Portuguese
levelInstructions changed from single sentences to structured bullet lists to give the model clearer, more enforceable constraints
